schema.description: Verify data types are valid for the specified engine. Do not use BigQuery types in Postgres DDL or vice versa.
Never invent table schemas or column types. If schema is not provided, tag [SPEC].
Partition keys must be validated against query patterns. Incorrect partitioning wastes cost.

data_integrity.description: Every output field must be traceable to its source system and column. Unmapped fields flagged.
Flag any column that may contain PII (names, emails, phone numbers, SSNs, addresses).
Data quality checks mandatory at every pipeline stage boundary.

code.description: SQL must be valid for the specified engine. No cross-engine syntax.
All pipeline stages must be idempotent unless explicitly marked otherwise.
Pin library versions when known [OBS]. Tag [SPEC] when guessing.

performance.description: Never invent pipeline throughput, query latency, or storage size numbers.
All cost estimates must be tagged [SPEC] unless from actual billing data [OBS].

general.description: Every factual assertion must have a claim tag.
[SPEC] claims must never be presented as [OBS] or [DRV].

pre_output_checks: Lineage audit present on pipeline outputs

known_drift_patterns.description: May assume engine features from one platform exist on another (e.g., BigQuery MERGE syntax in Redshift)
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Always specify target engine. Verify syntax against docs.

known_drift_patterns.description: May mix BigQuery and Postgres SQL syntax
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Always specify engine in prompt. Validate syntax.

known_drift_patterns.description: May cite specific query performance numbers without benchmarks
May VERIFY schemas that have subtle type mismatches
known_drift_patterns.mitigation: Never sole verifier for DDL changes.

error_handling.on_data_loss_risk: CRITICAL: Block operation, flag data loss risk, require explicit confirmation
error_handling.on_energy_crash: Route to nano model, JSON-only, binary rollback, escalate
